65.reason: [Error: When j reaches i-1, res[j+1] accesses out-of-bounds memory, causing undefined behavior. Also, the logic for combining like terms is flawed; it modifies res[j+1] but then prints res[j] after skipping, which may skip or duplicate terms.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: When j reaches i-1, res[j+1] accesses out-of-bounds memory, causing undefined behavior. Also, the logic for combining like terms is flawed; it modifies res[j+1] but then prints res[j] after skipping, which may skip or duplicate terms.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  Merging logic is flawed: it accumulates coefficients into res[j+1] but prints res[j] after incrementing j, which can skip terms or print incorrect merged values. Zero coefficients after merging are also not filtered out.

70.reason: Missing closing brace '}' for this while loop, causing a compilation error due to mismatched braces.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Same out-of-bounds issue; also the inner while loop may cause j to exceed i-1, leading to further out-of-bounds access.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing closing brace '}' for this while loop, causing a compilation error due to mismatched braces.

26.reason: Reads entire input until EOF, so polynomial1 will include all terms from both polynomials; should stop at newline per polynomial.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Reads entire input until EOF, so polynomial1 will include all terms from both polynomials; should stop at newline per polynomial.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [reads until EOF instead of newline, causing the second polynomial to be empty]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  scanf skips whitespace including newlines, so it will consume both polynomials into the first list instead of stopping at the newline separator between inputs.

66.reason: After addition, res[j] is not zeroed, so if later printed (through different branch), duplicate output may occur.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  After addition, res[j] is not zeroed, so if later printed (through different branch), duplicate output may occur.

45.reason: [fixed-size array may overflow if more than 100 terms, though not the core error]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [fixed-size array may overflow if more than 100 terms, though not the core error]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  Fixed size array may cause buffer overflow if the number of product terms exceeds 100; dynamic allocation or a larger size is safer.

9.reason: Casting away const qualifier from 'a' may cause compilation warnings/errors in strict environments; should use 'const item *a1 = a;'

74.reason: Redundant: 'continue' inside this while loop only jumps to the loop's condition check, which happens naturally. It does not skip to the next iteration of the outer loop.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  Redundant: 'continue' inside this while loop only jumps to the loop's condition check, which happens naturally. It does not skip to the next iteration of the outer loop.
